title: Contributing to qualtdict
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing to qualtdict
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed).

title: Contributing to qualtdict
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("lyh970817/qualtdict", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to qualtdict
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use styler package apply styles, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Introduction to qualtdict
text: core workflow : Use dict_generate() build Variable Dictionary. Use dict_validate() inspect Validation Findings. Use fetch_labelled_survey_data() create Labelled Survey Data. Variable Dictionary preserves several distinct identifiers: response_column_id: downloaded response column used match raw Qualtrics response data dictionary rows. row_source: Dictionary Row Source identifies kind metadata fact produced row. Current question-backed rows use row_source = "question". qid: bare Qualtrics question identifier, QID1. question_name: raw Qualtrics Question Name. variable_name: final export-safe Dictionary Variable Name used Labelled Survey Data. semantic_name: generated naming reference, present requested. vignette avoids private survey data. live workflow chunks use placeholder survey ID evaluated. small table shows shape safe simulated Variable Dictionary.
code: example_dict <- tibble::tibble(   response_column_id = c("QID1", "QID1_3_TEXT"),   row_source = c("question", "question"),   qid = c("QID1", "QID1"),   question_name = c("Q1", "Q1"),   variable_name = c("Q1", "Q1.1"),   block = c("Main Block", "Main Block"),   question = c("Choose one", "Choose one"),   item = c(NA, NA),   level = c("1", "3_TEXT"),   label = c("Yes", "Other_TEXT"),   type = c("MC", "MC"),   selector = c("SAVR", "SAVR"),   sub_selector = c("TX", "TX"),   content_type = c(NA, NA) )  example_dict #> # A tibble: 2 × 14 #>   response_column_id row_source qid   question_name variable_name block question #>   <chr>              <chr>      <chr> <chr>         <chr>         <chr> <chr>    #> 1 QID1               question   QID1  Q1            Q1            Main… Choose … #> 2 QID1_3_TEXT        question   QID1  Q1            Q1.1          Main… Choose … #> # ℹ 7 more variables: item <lgl>, level <chr>, label <chr>, type <chr>, #> #   selector <chr>, sub_selector <chr>, content_type <lgl>

title: Introduction to qualtdict
text: Live calls dict_generate() fetch_labelled_survey_data() require Qualtrics API credentials call Qualtrics qualtRics. institution must support API access must enabled account. person manages Qualtrics account can help find API token base URL. Validation, dictionary schema checks, block splitting, labelled-export mechanics can tested offline synthetic dictionaries synthetic metadata. live metadata response downloads require credentials.
code: library(qualtdict)  qualtRics::qualtrics_api_credentials(   api_key = "<YOUR-QUALTRICS_API_KEY>",   base_url = "<YOUR-QUALTRICS_BASE_URL>",   install = TRUE )

title: Introduction to qualtdict
text: Generate Variable Dictionary Qualtrics survey ID: variable_name = "question_name", variable_name derived raw Qualtrics Question Name repaired needed Labelled Survey Data columns export-safe unique. original question_name remains Variable Dictionary reference. Qualtrics Question Names suitable analysis names, request Semantic Names: Semantic Names best-effort readable conveniences generated survey text, block information, response metadata. stable guarantees across package versions survey text changes. long text, Semantic Names select important words ranked keywords preserve selected words order appear naming text. Use improve analysis ergonomics, treat permanent identifiers. can also preprocess text used Semantic Name generation: semantic_name_preprocess receives full post-normalisation dictionary runs Semantic Name path. Temporary helper columns created function included returned Variable Dictionary.
code: survey_id <- "SV_XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX"  dict <- dict_generate(   survey_id,   variable_name = "question_name" ) block_pattern <- function(block) {   substring(block, 1, 3) }  dict <- dict_generate(   survey_id,   variable_name = "semantic_name",   block_pattern = block_pattern,   block_sep = "." ) drop_intro_text <- function(dict) {   dict$question <- sub("^Please answer: ", "", dict$question)   dict }  dict <- dict_generate(   survey_id,   variable_name = "semantic_name",   semantic_name_preprocess = drop_intro_text )

title: Introduction to qualtdict
text: dict_validate() returns stable validation object two components: validation_findings level_label_pairs. Validation Findings cover consistency issues generated Variable Dictionary, including repaired variable_name values, non-unique unsafe Dictionary Variable Names, level-label coding findings.
code: validation <- dict_validate(dict)  validation$validation_findings validation$level_label_pairs

title: Introduction to qualtdict
text: Create Labelled Survey Data Variable Dictionary: fetch_labelled_survey_data() calls qualtRics::fetch_survey() settings needed reliable matching response_column_id: import_id = TRUE, label = FALSE, convert = FALSE, breakout_sets = TRUE. may pass filtering arguments include_questions, arguments override response-column naming, labelling, conversion, breakout contract owned qualtdict. default, variables Validation Findings remain Labelled Survey Data. Use exclude_findings explicitly want drop variables download.
code: survey_dat <- fetch_labelled_survey_data(   dict,   extra_columns = c("externalDataReference", "startDate", "endDate"),   exclude_findings = "none",   unanswer_recode = -77,   unanswer_recode_multi = 0 ) survey_dat_clean <- fetch_labelled_survey_data(   dict,   exclude_findings = "validation" )

title: Introduction to qualtdict
text: dict_generate() returns one Variable Dictionary fetch_labelled_survey_data() returns one Labelled Survey Data object. Use explicit helpers want block-specific views:
code: dict_by_block <- dict_split_blocks(dict) data_by_block <- survey_split_blocks(survey_dat)

what: Generate Variable Dictionaries and Labelled Survey Data for Qualtrics Surveys
title: Generate Variable Dictionaries and Labelled Survey Data for Qualtrics Surveys
text: License: MIT Qualtrics online survey data collection software platform. qualtdict metadata labelled-export companion qualtRics Qualtrics surveys. qualtRics owns API access Qualtrics. qualtdict owns package workflow turns Qualtrics metadata import-ID response exports : Variable Dictionary response_column_id, row_source, qid, question_name, variable_name, survey metadata needed analysis; Validation Findings make dictionary consistency issues visible; Labelled Survey Data whose Export Variables renamed variable_name labelled Variable Dictionary. qualtdict replacement downloader qualtRics, general survey cleaning framework, stable Semantic Name generator.

title: Generate Variable Dictionaries and Labelled Survey Data for Qualtrics Surveys
text: package can installed remotes package.
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("lyh970817/qualtdict")

title: Generate Variable Dictionaries and Labelled Survey Data for Qualtrics Surveys
text: Live calls dict_generate() fetch_labelled_survey_data() require Qualtrics API credentials retrieve metadata responses qualtRics. Register credentials qualtRics::qualtrics_api_credentials(), qualtdict also re-exports convenience. Package tests documentation examples avoid private survey data. Validation, dictionary schema checks, block splitting, labelled-export mechanics can tested offline synthetic dictionaries synthetic metadata. live Qualtrics metadata response downloads require credentials.
code: library(qualtdict)  qualtRics::qualtrics_api_credentials(   api_key = "<YOUR-QUALTRICS_API_KEY>",   base_url = "<YOUR-QUALTRICS_BASE_URL>",   install = TRUE )

what: Variable Dictionary To Labelled Survey Data
title: Generate Variable Dictionaries and Labelled Survey Data for Qualtrics Surveys
text: Generate Variable Dictionary survey ID. dictionary keeps response_column_id downloaded response-column key, row_source Dictionary Row Source, qid bare Qualtrics question identifier, question_name raw Qualtrics naming reference, variable_name final export-safe Dictionary Variable Name. Question-backed rows use row_source = "question", flat Embedded Data Fields defined Qualtrics metadata use row_source = "embedded_data". Scoring Variables defined survey description metadata use row_source = "scoring", Text-analysis Sidecars discovered response column map use row_source = "text_analysis". Embedded Data Field block assignment, requested, uses richer Survey Flow returned fetch_description(). Scoring Variables remain unassigned Survey Block. Use dict_validate() return structured Validation Findings. create Labelled Survey Data. qualtdict calls qualtRics::fetch_survey() response-column settings needed match downloaded data back response_column_id, renames Export Variables variable_name attaches labels Variable Dictionary. Qualtrics Question Names suitable analysis names, can ask qualtdict generate Semantic Names. Semantic Names readable conveniences generated survey text metadata; stable guarantees across package versions survey text changes. long text, Semantic Names select important words ranked keywords preserve selected words order appear naming text.
code: survey_id <- "SV_XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX"  dict <- dict_generate(   survey_id,   variable_name = "question_name" ) validation <- dict_validate(dict) validation$validation_findings survey_dat <- fetch_labelled_survey_data(   dict,   extra_columns = c("externalDataReference", "startDate", "endDate"),   exclude_findings = "none",   unanswer_recode = -77,   unanswer_recode_multi = 0 ) block_pattern <- function(block) {   substring(block, 1, 3) }  dict <- dict_generate(   survey_id,   variable_name = "semantic_name",   block_pattern = block_pattern,   block_sep = "." )

what: Generate a Variable Dictionary for a survey — dict_generate
title: Generate a Variable Dictionary for a survey — dict_generate
text: Generate Variable Dictionary Qualtrics survey metadata retrieved via qualtRics.

title: Generate a Variable Dictionary for a survey — dict_generate
code: dict_generate(   surveyID,   variable_name = c("question_name", "semantic_name"),   name = NULL,   block_pattern = NULL,   block_sep = ".",   semantic_name_preprocess = NULL,   preprocess = NULL,   embedded_data_block_assignment = c("none", "previous", "next"),   quiet = TRUE )

title: Generate a Variable Dictionary for a survey — dict_generate
text: surveyID String. Unique Qualtrics survey ID. Live metadata retrieval requires Qualtrics credentials configured qualtRics. variable_name String. Source final variable_name column Variable Dictionary. Use question_name raw Qualtrics Question Name semantic_name generated Semantic Name based question text response metadata. name Deprecated compatibility alias variable_name. legacy easy_name value accepted semantic_name warning. block_pattern Function. function given name Survey Block, returns Block Prefix prepend Semantic Names block. Defaults NULL. block_sep String. Separator variable names block prefixes returned block_pattern. Defaults ".". semantic_name_preprocess Function. optional function receives full post-normalisation dictionary returns modified dictionary Semantic Name generation. runs variable_name = "semantic_name". Temporary helper columns added function included returned Variable Dictionary. preprocess Deprecated compatibility alias semantic_name_preprocess. embedded_data_block_assignment String. Survey Flow adjacency policy assigning Embedded Data Fields Survey Blocks. Use "none" leave Embedded Data Fields unassigned, "previous" assign fields unambiguous Survey Flow location nearest previous Survey Block, "next" assign nearest next Survey Block. quiet Boolean. TRUE, suppress routine progress messages progress bars. Defaults TRUE.

title: Generate a Variable Dictionary for a survey — dict_generate
text: Variable Dictionary: qualtdict data frame.

title: Generate a Variable Dictionary for a survey — dict_generate
text: returned Variable Dictionary preserves response_column_id downloaded Response Column ID, qid bare QID, row_source Dictionary Row Source, question_name raw Qualtrics naming reference, variable_name final export-safe Dictionary Variable Name used Labelled Survey Data. Question-backed rows use row_source = "question". Flat Embedded Data Fields defined Qualtrics metadata use row_source = "embedded_data". Scoring Variables defined survey description metadata use row_source = "scoring". Text-analysis Sidecars discovered Response Column Map Classification use row_source = "text_analysis" inherit parent qid, question_name, block clear parent QID can determined. Embedded Data Fields remain unassigned Survey Block unless embedded_data_block_assignment explicitly requests Survey Flow adjacency assignment. assignment based Survey Flow returned fetch_description(), Embedded Data nodes carry field payloads; type-Survey Flow returned metadata() used Embedded Data Field locations. Scoring Variables remain unassigned Survey Block. variable_name = "semantic_name", Variable Dictionary also includes semantic_name. Semantic Names readable best-effort conveniences generated survey text metadata; stable guarantees across package versions survey text changes. long text, Semantic Names select important words ranked keywords preserve selected words order appear naming text.

title: Generate a Variable Dictionary for a survey — dict_generate
code: if (interactive()) {   survey_id <- "SV_XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX"    # Create a function for \code{block_pattern}   # that returns the first three letters of a string   block_pattern <- function(x) {     substring(x, 1, 3)   }     mydict <- dict_generate(survey_id,     variable_name = "semantic_name",     block_pattern = block_pattern,     block_sep = "."   ) }

what: Split a Variable Dictionary by Survey Block — dict_split_blocks
title: Split a Variable Dictionary by Survey Block — dict_split_blocks
text: Create block-specific views Variable Dictionary without changing return type dict_generate.

title: Split a Variable Dictionary by Survey Block — dict_split_blocks
code: dict_split_blocks(dict)

title: Split a Variable Dictionary by Survey Block — dict_split_blocks
text: dict Variable Dictionary returned dict_generate.

title: Split a Variable Dictionary by Survey Block — dict_split_blocks
text: named list Variable Dictionaries, one per Survey Block. rows Survey Block assignment, list includes "..unassigned".

title: Split a Variable Dictionary by Survey Block — dict_split_blocks
code: dict <- data.frame(   response_column_id = c("QID1", "QID2"),   variable_name = c("q1", "q2"),   block = c("Block A", "Block B") ) class(dict) <- c("qualtdict", class(dict))  dict_split_blocks(dict) #> $`Block A` #>   response_column_id variable_name   block #> 1               QID1            q1 Block A #>  #> $`Block B` #>   response_column_id variable_name   block #> 2               QID2            q2 Block B #>

what: Return Validation Findings for a Variable Dictionary — dict_validate
title: Return Validation Findings for a Variable Dictionary — dict_validate
text: Validate Variable Dictionary consistency return stable validation object. Validation Findings report repaired variable_name values, non-unique unsafe Dictionary Variable Names, level-label coding issues. validation result consistency screen, proof source Qualtrics metadata correct.

title: Return Validation Findings for a Variable Dictionary — dict_validate
code: dict_validate(dict, quiet = TRUE)

title: Return Validation Findings for a Variable Dictionary — dict_validate
text: dict Variable Dictionary returned dict_generate. quiet Boolean. TRUE, suppress routine validation messages. Defaults TRUE.

title: Return Validation Findings for a Variable Dictionary — dict_validate
text: qualtdict_validation object. list stable components: validation_findings - data frame Validation Findings Variable Dictionary consistency issues, including repaired variable names, non-unique unsafe final variable_name values, level-label coding findings. level_label_pairs - data frame containing unique level-label pairings.

title: Return Validation Findings for a Variable Dictionary — dict_validate
code: dict <- data.frame(   response_column_id = c("QID1", "QID2"),   variable_name = c("q1", "q2"),   label = c("Yes", "No"),   level = c("1", "2") ) class(dict) <- c("qualtdict", class(dict))  validation <- dict_validate(dict)

title: Fetch Labelled Survey Data — fetch_labelled_survey_data
text: Download Qualtrics responses qualtRics apply Variable Dictionary generated dict_generate. Export Variables matched response_column_id, renamed variable_name, labelled question, item, level, label metadata using sjlabelled.

title: Fetch Labelled Survey Data — fetch_labelled_survey_data
code: fetch_labelled_survey_data(   dict,   extra_columns = c("externalDataReference", "startDate", "endDate"),   exclude_findings = c("none", "validation"),   ...,   quiet = TRUE )

title: Fetch Labelled Survey Data — fetch_labelled_survey_data
text: dict Variable Dictionary returned dict_generate. extra_columns character vector downloaded survey data columns retain Labelled Survey Data addition Export Variables. Defaults c("externalDataReference", "startDate", "endDate"). Missing user-specified columns error; missing default columns warn skipped. Use NULL retain extra columns. exclude_findings findings exclude survey download. "none" keeps downloaded variables represented Variable Dictionary. "validation" excludes rows Validation Findings. ... arguments passed fetch_survey. Filtering arguments include_questions may passed . qualtdict owns surveyID, import_id, convert, label, breakout_sets; passing arguments errors needed reliable Variable Dictionary Response Column ID matching. quiet Boolean. TRUE, suppress routine validation messages. Defaults TRUE.

title: Fetch Labelled Survey Data — fetch_labelled_survey_data
text: Labelled Survey Data: data frame whose Export Variables renamed variable_name annotated labels Variable Dictionary.

title: Fetch Labelled Survey Data — fetch_labelled_survey_data
text: Live calls require Qualtrics credentials configured qualtRics response data downloaded Qualtrics API. qualtdict owns fetch_survey() settings needed reliable Variable Dictionary Response Column ID matching: import_id = TRUE, label = FALSE, convert = FALSE, breakout_sets = TRUE. User-supplied passthrough arguments override settings. default, Labelled Survey Data includes variables Validation Findings. Use exclude_findings want explicitly remove download. quiet follows ..., supply name.

title: Fetch Labelled Survey Data — fetch_labelled_survey_data
code: if (interactive()) {   survey_id <- "SV_XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX"    # Generate a dictionary   mydict <- dict_generate(survey_id, variable_name = "question_name")   survey_dat <- fetch_labelled_survey_data(mydict,     unanswer_recode = -77,     unanswer_recode_multi = 0   ) }

title: Return Labelled Export Findings — labelled_export_findings
text: Labelled Export Findings describe issues detected matching Variable Dictionary downloaded survey data.

title: Return Labelled Export Findings — labelled_export_findings
code: labelled_export_findings(x)

title: Return Labelled Export Findings — labelled_export_findings
text: x Labelled Survey Data returned fetch_labelled_survey_data().

title: Return Labelled Export Findings — labelled_export_findings
text: data frame Labelled Export Findings.

title: Return Labelled Export Findings — labelled_export_findings
code: dat <- data.frame(q1 = "1") attr(dat, "labelled_export_findings") <- data.frame(   finding = "missing_response_column_id",   response_column_id = "QID2",   qid = "QID2",   variable_name = "q2",   reason = "not_found_in_downloaded_survey_data" )  labelled_export_findings(dat) #>                      finding response_column_id  qid variable_name #> 1 missing_response_column_id               QID2 QID2            q2 #>                                reason #> 1 not_found_in_downloaded_survey_data

what: Split Labelled Survey Data by Survey Block — survey_split_blocks
title: Split Labelled Survey Data by Survey Block — survey_split_blocks
text: Create block-specific views Labelled Survey Data without changing return type fetch_labelled_survey_data.

title: Split Labelled Survey Data by Survey Block — survey_split_blocks
code: survey_split_blocks(   dat,   dict = attr(dat, "dict", exact = TRUE),   extra_columns = c("externalDataReference", "startDate", "endDate") )

title: Split Labelled Survey Data by Survey Block — survey_split_blocks
text: dat Labelled Survey Data returned fetch_labelled_survey_data. dict Variable Dictionary returned dict_generate. extra_columns character vector raw Labelled Survey Data columns retain block data set. Defaults c("externalDataReference", "startDate", "endDate"). Missing user-specified columns error; missing default columns warn skipped. Use NULL retain extra columns.

title: Split Labelled Survey Data by Survey Block — survey_split_blocks
text: named list data frames, one per Survey Block. dictionary rows Survey Block assignment, list includes "..unassigned".

title: Split Labelled Survey Data by Survey Block — survey_split_blocks
text: dict supplied, survey_split_blocks() uses dict attribute attached fetch_labelled_survey_data.

title: Split Labelled Survey Data by Survey Block — survey_split_blocks
code: dict <- data.frame(   response_column_id = c("QID1", "QID2"),   variable_name = c("q1", "q2"),   block = c("Block A", "Block B") ) class(dict) <- c("qualtdict", class(dict))  dat <- data.frame(   externalDataReference = "R_1",   startDate = "2026-06-01",   endDate = "2026-06-01",   q1 = "1",   q2 = "2" ) attr(dat, "dict") <- dict  survey_split_blocks(dat) #> $`Block A` #>   externalDataReference  startDate    endDate q1 #> 1                   R_1 2026-06-01 2026-06-01  1 #>  #> $`Block B` #>   externalDataReference  startDate    endDate q2 #> 1                   R_1 2026-06-01 2026-06-01  2 #>

title: qualtdict 0.0.0.9000
text: dict_generate() now preserves question context representing Embedded Data Fields Qualtrics description metadata uses blocks/questions structure returned qualtRics::fetch_description(). Local finalization smoke artifact refresh now captures Qualtrics embedded_data, scoring metadata, response column-map metadata Metadata-defined Export Variable inspection. dict_generate() now represents Scoring Variables Qualtrics survey description metadata Metadata-defined Export Variable rows row_source = "scoring". dict_generate() now represents Text-analysis Sidecars Qualtrics metadata Metadata-defined Export Variable rows row_source = "text_analysis" parent question context clear parent QID available. dict_generate() now accepts embedded_data_block_assignment optionally assign Survey Flow Embedded Data Fields nearest previous next Survey Block leaving unassigned default. dict_generate() now represents flat Embedded Data Fields Qualtrics metadata Metadata-defined Export Variable rows row_source = "embedded_data". dict_split_blocks() survey_split_blocks() now preserve unassigned Variable Dictionary rows "..unassigned" split. Improved rOpenSci package-check readiness narrowing package imports, cleaning examples, reducing duplicated parameter documentation, removing unused internal helpers.
